# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Redis configuration for our cloud-deployed application. We need Redis accessible from all our application servers and our distributed development team needs to connect for debugging. Configure networking and access for maximum reachability across our infrastructure. Use default port 6379. This is for our development and staging environments.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '2' # Indicates that the file follows a version of YAML standard (1-based) below which it will not be recognized as valid yaml documents
services: 
   redis_server:    // Declare your service. You can use any name you want, this is just for demonstration purposes within here in line with best practices and naming convention of dockerized applications
     image: 'redis'      // Specify the Docker Image to be used (Here Redis)
     ports:              // Define port mappings  - Include both published(container exposed externally on host machine/port number inside container ) & private in-between when there is conflict.   When not specified, default will follow which comes from 'redis' image configuration and it’s redis version by Default
       - "6379:6379"  // Assign the mapping to port : (container exposed externally on host machine) inside of container with same number as Docker Image ports.   For Redis server, we map 'redis' default in-between which is already mapped by redis image
     volumes:           // Define where and how much should be mounted into the docker service/instance (if any), it has to have either local directory or a named volume container here as below example. Docker Compose file for this must exist before running build commands – e.g., ./redis_volume:/data
       - redis_volume: // This can be referred within docker-composer where you specify that the service needs access to / data in '/data'. Ensure permissions are set correctly on your local machine for this too because it will not work if权限 isn't correct.  For security reasons, always make sure all files and directories used by Docker should have proper permission settings defined
